problem: What material is biocompatible, conductive, and flexible enough for long-term brain implants?
mechanism: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure)
hypothesis: What material is biocompatible, conductive, and flexible enough for long-term brain implants? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Two-dimensional. Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure) directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Mechanical testing (tensile, fatigue) with 17 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: We cannot solve problems with the same thinking that created them. — Einstein paraphrase
